research_question: After the guard-only VSUMD60 family seed failed, can a second restrained family seed that changes only the portfolio extraction rule improve benchmark-relative quality without giving back too much of the confirmed standalone VSUMD60 edge?

time_partition_plan.note: The frozen 2022-2025 test window remains excluded by construction through the trainval-only snapshot.

portfolio_extraction_contract.extraction_definition: Keep the VSUMD60 score and TopK universe unchanged. Within each signal_date, apply a linear rank-decay extraction across the frozen TopK head so rank 1 receives the highest deterministic multiplier and rank 10 the lowest, then renormalize active selected weights to sum to 1.0.

constants_frozen.ranking_eligibility_guard: inherit standalone confirmatory VSUMD60 reference with no additional liquidity guard
constants_frozen.portfolio_refresh_rule: inherit current full daily refresh from the standalone confirmatory reference unless explicitly superseded in a later seed round

decision_rule.interpretation: This seed is allowed to increase head extraction only if it preserves or improves standalone edge, does not materially worsen drawdown, and does not create an obvious tradability or cost regression.

design_constraints: Only the portfolio extraction rule may change in this seed round.
Do not reopen the failed Alpha158-to-v18 direct overlay path.
Do not add any second atomic signal.
Do not change score field, ranking direction, TopK width, refresh rule, benchmark, or execution semantics in this round.
disallowed_changes: Do not change the VSUMD60 score field or ranking direction.
Do not add multi-signal blends or overlays.
Do not change the snapshot, contract, or validation window after registration.
Do not add a liquidity guard back into this round.
Do not compare only to v18; the primary reference for this round must be the confirmatory standalone VSUMD60 winner.
notes: This prereg defines the second restrained family-seed transition from the confirmed standalone VSUMD60 winner.
The first guard-only seed failed, so this round changes exactly one different core dimension: portfolio extraction.
